A.notes: Strong oracle alignment: strict regex with boundaries rejects garbage like 'a@.com'; dedup is opt-in (unique=False default), so no unrequested normalization. All hidden assertions pass. Very thorough but the long Brief plus extensive self-tests is somewhat over-engineered for a one-line ask.

B.notes: Hits must-haves and assertions still pass on lowercase inputs, but performs unrequested normalization (lowercasing + dedup) as the only behavior with no opt-out — directly hits a 'should avoid' even though it's documented. Brief overhead is heavy for a trivial task.

C.notes: Tight, minimal solution that does exactly what was asked: function + standard regex + findall. No unrequested normalization, no clarification questions, briefly flags potential extensions (RFC 5322, dedup, lowercase). All hidden assertions pass; regex is the common pragmatic one.

D.notes: Clean function with type hints, empty/None-safe guard, no unrequested normalization, mentions dedup as optional follow-up. All hidden assertions pass. Slightly more commentary than necessary but well within scope.

E.notes: Solid code and well-documented, but performs unrequested dedup (case-insensitive) as default with no opt-out, which is a 'should avoid' item even though flagged. Heavy Brief for a simple ask. Hidden assertions still pass because test inputs have no duplicates.
